explanation.one_sentence: This repository is a full Depth Anything 3 analysis snapshot describing a Python-based 3D inference system that predicts depth and camera information, serves it through multiple interfaces, and exports results for downstream geometry workflows.
what_it_does: Runs Depth Anything 3 inference on images, videos, and related visual inputs
Exposes the model through a Python API, CLI, Gradio app, and FastAPI backend
Produces depth, confidence, pose, and camera outputs plus exportable 3D artifacts
Extends the base system with a streaming pipeline for chunked long-video reconstruction and loop-aware alignment

how_it_is_organized.main_api: src/depth_anything_3/api.py is the central entry point into configs, models, I/O, and export helpers
how_it_is_organized.interfaces: src/depth_anything_3/cli.py and src/depth_anything_3/app/gradio_app.py provide command-line and interactive user surfaces
how_it_is_organized.services: src/depth_anything_3/services contains backend orchestration, gallery logic, and inference dispatch
how_it_is_organized.model_core: src/depth_anything_3/model contains the DA3 model family, DINOv2 backbone components, heads, and rendering adapters
how_it_is_organized.utilities: src/depth_anything_3/utils contains geometry, export, visualization, and memory helpers
how_it_is_organized.streaming: da3_streaming adds long-sequence chunking, loop detection, and Sim3 alignment

explanation.how_to_think_about_it: Treat the repo as a shared inference engine with several operator surfaces around it: the API is the center, the CLI/UI/backend are wrappers, and the streaming subtree is a specialized extension for long videos.
important_details: The project is deeper and more modular than a typical demo repo; most complexity lives in model and utility layers.
Export support is a first-class concern, with helpers for formats and visual outputs used in broader 3D pipelines.
The backend exists to keep models warm in memory, which matters for expensive GPU initialization and repeated jobs.
The available analysis is based on a `preprep-repo` snapshot rather than direct raw-source inspection.
likely_use_cases: Long-video scene analysis with loop-aware alignment
